macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market rebounds slightly as Bitcoin and Ethereum dip
items.summary: The total crypto market cap rose modestly to about $2.68 trillion even as Bitcoin slipped 1.76% to around $78,970 and Ethereum fell 0.81% to about $2,225. The report also noted strong meme-coin moves, with GPM, CAT, and GME among the day’s biggest gainers.

items.title: Dogecoin climbs as spot and futures volume surge
items.summary: Dogecoin outperformed the broader market after spot volume jumped 63% and futures volume rose 71%, signaling renewed trader interest despite weak overall crypto sentiment. The coverage said bullish technical indicators raised expectations for a potential move toward $0.155.

items.title: About $410 million liquidated from crypto markets in 24 hours
items.summary: A sharp volatility spike triggered roughly $410 million in forced liquidations across leveraged crypto positions over the past day. The report highlighted continued risk in derivatives trading as price swings unsettled sentiment.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $80,000 amid risk-off selling
items.summary: Bitcoin reportedly dropped to around $77,614 as markets reacted to geopolitical concerns and broader risk-off sentiment. The coverage also pointed to a potential short squeeze if BTC reclaims key resistance near $81,818.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Tom's Hardware AI roundup highlights major industry developments
items.summary: Tom's Hardware’s artificial intelligence coverage flags several recent developments, including Google reportedly discussing SpaceX as a launch partner for its Project Suncatcher orbital data centers and a growing wave of data-center related policy and infrastructure issues. The roundup also mentions mixed-architecture AI hardware concerns, restricted Nvidia hardware smuggling networks, and a grey-market resale of Anthropic Claude API access in China.

items.title: DeepLearning.AI search results point to a Google AI mammogram study update
items.summary: DeepLearning.AI’s The Batch search results reference a piece on AI mammogram diagnosis under real-world conditions, describing two studies that test Google’s breast cancer detection models in clinical settings. The result suggests continued progress in medical AI validation and deployment.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Yuji Horii teases Dragon Quest updates, including the return of a long-lost SRPG
items.summary: A recent gaming news roundup says Dragon Quest creator Yuji Horii shared new details on the series’ ongoing projects, including confirmation that the previously shelved strategy RPG Lost Order is still in development. The discussion also revisited several Dragon Quest titles and future franchise plans.

items.title: Trails from Zero/Azure collection reportedly coming to PS5 and Switch 2 this fall
items.summary: The same roundup reports that The Legend of Heroes: Trails from Zero and Trails to Azure are set to arrive as a bundle for PS5 and Switch 2 this fall. According to the video, the release will be sold digitally or as a physical collection by NIS America.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
